entry.title: SAP Maintenance Resource Scheduling Part -1 ( With apps and Step By Step guide)
entry.content:
Use Case:

This sprawling text gives you the crisp idea to maintain one’s available resource capacities in their optimum capacity. Also provides Information regarding a different scenario for resource scheduling, through Various available functionalities.

  • Order operations can be scheduled based on the priority/criticality of the work.
  • Schedules can be created for a target week by selecting operations from multiple maintenance orders.
  • Refinement of the schedules can be done based on the utilization forecast of the work centers for the week.

Prema facially to take advantage of Maintenance Resource Schedule through Fiori launchpad following apps are needs to be adopted in system with giving appropriate roles and authorization to authenticated End-User.  

  1. Resource Scheduling for Maintenance Planners (App ID: F2227).
  2. Manage Schedule (App ID: F3326).
  3. Maintenance Scheduling Board (App ID: F2603).
  4. Assign Maintenance Order Operation (App ID: F3423).

In the upcoming para will be dealing section wise functionalities in Maintenance Resource Schedule through Fiori launchpad Apps. Such as assign work center, create Work Center Team, create planning bucket, manage schedule, Freeze Schedule.

Apps look like as,

A. Assign Work Centre:

  1. To assign work Centre, Access the Resource Scheduling for Maintenance Planners (F2227) app.
  2. Choose the user profile button on the top right-hand corner of the screen.
  3. Choose My Work Centers.

4.Add a row (+) and enter the name of the work center that you want to assign to yourself.
5.Select the right work center from the list of proposed work centers
6.Choose Assign.


You can also change capacities likewise:

B. Manage Schedule:

B.1. Create Schedule:

  1. Choose the Resource Scheduling for Maintenance Planners tile.
  2. Enter the required filter values and choose the Go button.
  3. Choose the header area of Work Center Utilization card.
  4. Select due order operations by selecting the checkbox.
  5. Choose Add to Schedule. A dialog box opens.

  1. Select Create New Schedule and enter a schedule name and the time period (define timeframe for the schedule).
  2. Choose Create and Open Schedule. 

    Create New Schedule: Test Schedule

In the similar way new schedule will display on screen.

B.2. Level Work Centre Utilization

  1. Check the weekly utilization forecast on the List View tab.
  2. If work center utilization is not yet optimal, use one of the following options:

Add more due order operations. To do so, use the back button and follow these steps:

  • Select a few more due order operations by selecting the checkbox.
  • Choose Add to Schedule. A dialog box opens.
  • Select the radio button Add to Existing Schedule and then select the schedule created in previous step.
  • Choose Add and Open Schedule.

Remove order operations from the schedule if there's not enough capacity to complete them in the target week. To do so, follow these steps on the Manage Schedule page:

  • Select the operation on the List View tab.
  • Choose the three dots and choose the Remove button.

3. To move order operations to another work center, select the operation by marking the checkbox and choose the required work center from the Change Work Center dropdown list.

B.3. Refine Schedule

  1. In the Manage Schedules (F3326) app, choose Refine Schedule to start planning at day level.
  2. Select the order operations whose start day you want to set by marking the checkbox.
  3. Schedule the selected order operations by using one of the following options:

Choose Schedule Automatically.

The app finds a start day and time slot for each order operation.

An information popup is displayed. Select on Do not show again and choose OK.

  1. Choose Set Start Date and Time.

B.4. Dispatch Order Operations

  1. Choose the arrow on the Freeze Schedule button.
  2. Choose Save and Dispatch Operations. The app tries to dispatch all scheduled operations.

B.5. Cancel the Dispatch OR Change Start Date of Dispatched Operations.

  1. Open the Resource Scheduling for Maintenance Planners (F2227) app.
  2. Choose the header area of the Work Center Utilization card.
  3. The Manage Work Center Utilization app opens.
  4. Filter the list of order operations for the order operations whose dispatch you want to cancel.
  5. Select the relevant order operations by marking the checkbox.
  6. Choose Cancel Dispatch.
  7. Select the relevant order operations by marking the checkbox.
  8. Choose Change.
  9. Enter a new start date (and, if required, time).
  10. To immediately dispatch the operation, select the relevant value in the Trigger or Cancel Dispatch field.
  11. Choose Change.

entry.title: SAP Academic Community Conference Armenia
entry.content:
The SAP academic community cordially invites you to participate in “The Academic Integration Approaches to SAP Solutions” conference held on 22 February, 2024, in the Center for Digital Transformation and Data Analyses at the Armenian State University of Armenia (ASUE). The agenda includes welcome remarks by ASUE Rector Diana Galoyan, SAP University Alliance Partners Director Michael Nurenberg and Coordinator for the Center for Digital Transformation and Data Analyses Sos Khachikyan, as well as presentations by business representatives and academic partners.

🖥️ Where?  Faculty of Computer Science and Statistics, Center for Digital Transformation and Data Analyses

🗺️ Address: 77 P. Sevak Street, 0069 Yerevan, Armenia

🔗 Link: https://events.sap.com/sap-acc-global-website/en/home

entry.title: SAP Maintenance Resource Scheduling Part -2 ( With apps and Step By Step guide)
entry.content:
This Blog is a continuation of the Blog SAP Maintenance Resource Scheduling Part -1 ( With apps and Step By Step guide).

In the upcoming para will be dealing section wise functionalities in Maintenance Resource Schedule through Fiori launchpad Apps. Such as assign person responsible to order operations, Work with the maintenance scheduling board and Work with maintenance schedule for assets along with Work Centre Utilization by priority.


C. Assign Person Responsible to Order Operations

D. Work with the Maintenance Scheduling Board

 

C.  Assign Person Responsible to Order Operations:

C.1. Assign Person Responsible

To assign a person responsible, follow these steps:

  1. Choose the Assign Maintenance Order Operations tile.
  2. In the filter bar, in the Assignment Status field, enter Unassigned.
  3. Choose Go.
  4. Select the order operations to which you want to assign a person responsible by marking the checkbox.
  5. Choose Assign.
  6. Enter the name of the person that you want to assign and select a name from the list that appears, or you can choose the Filter button and select people based on the performing work center.
  7. Choose Save.

C.2. Unassign Person Responsible

To take back the assignment of a person responsible, follow these steps:

  1. Select the relevant operations by marking the checkbox.
  2. Choose Unassign.
  3. Choose Save.

D. Work with the Maintenance Scheduling Board:

 

  1. Choose Maintenance Scheduling Board (F2603) tile.
  2. Enter the required filter values and choose Go.
  3. Expand the table entries to see order operations and suboperations included in an order.
  4. A Gantt chart will open with the list of orders, using various functionalities you can make basic changes as well.

In this way one can schedule maintenance order by managing is available capacities up to their maximum capacity (utility with profit making). Next picturesque will elaborate more on graphical representations. Here by order priority and Work Centre. One can go with other filters as well.

Utilization By priority:

entry.title: Suppliers should merge all electronic pipelines into an Omni-channel strategy.
entry.content:
Expanding upon the blog on how to differentiate within a marketplace...

Omni-channel sales for B2B (business-to-business) refers to a sales strategy that integrates multiple channels and touchpoints to provide customers a seamless and consistent buying experience. It involves utilizing various online and offline channels, such as websites, mobile apps, social media, email, phone, and in-person interactions, to engage with potential buyers and facilitate their purchasing journey.  Historically, SAP Ariba has focused solely on a simple commodity catalog (either cif or punchout) with pre-defined pricing and ignored the broader multi-channel needs of B2B procurement.  Need a widget, find a widget, buy a widget.  Omni-channel sales blend the multiple engagement points and value-added/assisted needs of complex goods and services.  This is seen in significant non-catalog ordering on SBN for Simple & Complex Services and Complex Goods.

Here are some key aspects of omnichannel sales for B2B:

  • Multiple Channels: B2B companies leverage various channels to reach their target audience. This includes having a solid online presence through websites, e-commerce platforms, and mobile apps, as well as utilizing offline channels like brick-and-mortar stores, quoting, call centers, and direct sales.
  • Consistent Messaging: Regardless of the channel, omnichannel sales ensure consistent messaging and branding. This helps build customer trust and familiarity, as they receive the same information and experience across different touchpoints.  Trading Partner Portal remains key link across options.
  • Seamless Integration: Omni-channel sales focuses on integrating different channels and touchpoints to provide a seamless experience for customers. For example, a customer might research a product online, request a quote via email, and then finalize the purchase as a non-catalog order via Ariba.  Reminder: over 80% of all network transactions are non-catalog today.   The goal is to make the transition between channels as smooth as possible, allowing customers to pick up where they left off without any disruption.  Syncing chat, quote integration, and punch-in helps address gaps.
  • Personalization: Omni-channel sales aims to personalize the buying experience based on customer preferences and behavior. By collecting and analyzing customer data from various channels, companies can tailor their messaging, offers, and recommendations to suit individual customer needs, increasing the likelihood of a value-added sale.
  • Customer Support: Omni-channel sales also extend to customer support and post-sales service. B2B companies provide support through multiple channels, such as phone, email, live chat, and social media, ensuring customers can reach out for assistance using their preferred method.  Today, engagement is limited to the collaboration feature and simple catalog buying. 
  • Data Integration: To effectively implement omni-channel sales, B2B companies need to integrate data from different channels and touchpoints. This gives them a holistic view of customer interactions, preferences, and buying patterns, enabling better decision-making and targeted marketing efforts.  Analytics, Analytics, Analytics.

Overall, omni-channel sales for B2B focuses on creating a seamless and personalized buying experience by integrating multiple channels and touchpoints. By doing so, companies can enhance customer satisfaction, improve sales conversion rates, and build stronger customer relationships.  Focusing on omni-channel will prevent that downgrade and potentially reverse the pattern.  The best way to support an omni-channel approach is with a punchout store.  A user should be able to request quotes and chat sessions and retrieve the quote via a punchout experience.  The seller controls how these channels interop through the experience.

entry.title: Additional SAP Community feeds 📡
entry.content:
In this blog post, I share some additional feeds that you can subscribe to, which expose some data in a similar granularity as the previous platform and might be useful for some of you.

Some of the new feeds shared below can provide you only with blog post information or just questions and answers per topic. This will be particularly useful if you use an RSS reader to keep up with news/articles/blog posts from different websites and you are interested in keeping track of specific topic(s). The feeds are generated by a little service I self-host, it might not be a long-term solution, but I'm planning on supporting this until the functionality is available out-of-the-box in the SAP Community new platform.

In a previous blog post I shared the different RSS feeds that are available out of the box in the new SAP Community platform - Exploring the RSS feeds of the new SAP Community platform

SAP Managed Tags (aka Topics)

The current platform doesn’t allow getting an RSS feed for an SAP Managed Tags, aka Topics. Not all topics available in SAP Community are included here. Below I’m sharing a sample of the topics available now and you’ll be able to see the full list here - topics.

You can suggest a new SAP Managed Tag feed by creating an issue in the GitHub repo.

Topic Blog posts feed Q&A feed Frequency

ABAP CloudURLURL3 hours
ABAP DevelopmentURLURL3 hours
ABAP RESTful Application Programming ModelURLURL3 hours
Cloud IntegrationURLURL3 hours
CloudURLURL3 hours
SAP BTP SecurityURLURL3 hours
SAP BTP, ABAP environmentURLURL3 hours
SAP BTP, Kyma runtimeURLURL3 hours
SAP HANA CloudURLURL3 hours
SAP HANAURLURL3 hours
SAP S/4HANA Public CloudURLURL3 hours
SAPUI5URLURL3 hours
SecurityURLURL3 hours
SAP TechEdURLURLTwice daily
User ExperienceURLURLTwice daily
User InterfaceURLURLTwice daily

SAP Community Members

Also, the current platform doesn’t allow getting an RSS feed for a community member. It will be quite an expensive task to generate a feed for every single member of the community, therefore I’m only including feeds for the Developer Advocates team and a couple of examples from SAP Mentors and SAP Champions. Below I’m sharing a sample of the community member feeds available now and you’ll be able to see the full list here - members.

You can suggest a new SAP Community Member feed by creating an issue in the GitHub repo.

Developer Advocates Username Feed Frequency

Ajay Sorengajay_sorengURL12 hours
Antonio MaradiagaajmaradiagaURL12 hours
Daniel WroblewskithecodesterURL12 hours
DJ AdamsqmacroURL12 hours
Ian Thainian_ThainURL12 hours
Josh Bentleyjosh_bentleyURL12 hours
Kevin RiedelsheimerkriedelsheimerURL12 hours
Mamikee KannehmamikeeURL12 hours
Michelle MoudyMichelleMoudyURL12 hours
Nicolai SchoenteichnicoschoenteichURL12 hours
Nora von ThenennoravonthenenURL12 hours
Rekha D RRekha_DRURL12 hours
Rich HeilmanRichHeilmanURL12 hours
Riley RaineyrileyraineyURL12 hours
Sheena M KsheenamkURL12 hours
Shilpa Shankarshilpa_shankarURL12 hours
Shrinivasan NeelamegameshrinivasanURL12 hours
Thomas Jungthomas_jungURL12 hours
Witalij RudnickiVitaliy-RURL12 hours

SAP Mentors Username Feed Frequency

Geert-Jan Klapsgeert-janklapsURL24 hours
Gregor WolfgregorwURL24 hours
Morten WittrockMortenWittrockURL24 hours
Tammy PowlasTammyPowlasURL24 hours
Volker BuzekvobuURL24 hours

SAP Champions Username Feed Frequency

David Ruiz Badiadavid_ruizbURL24 hours
Martin PankrazMartin-PankrazURL24 hours
Murali ShanmughamMurali_ShanURL24 hours
Mustafa BensanMustafaBensanURL24 hours
Sergio GuerreroSergioG_TXURL24 hours
Vladimirs SemikinsseVladimirsURL24 hours

I hope this helps filter out all the information that’s available in the SAP Community platform and that it can help you follow the information and members that you are really interested in.

First published on ajmaradiaga.com

entry.title: Unveiling Search Secrets: What You're Looking for in SAP Community
entry.content:
Now that we’ve navigated the great migration, it's time to peek behind the digital curtain and see how things are shaping up in our new home. As a search and SEO enthusiast, I'm curious about what queries driving traffic and user behavior. What are our members searching for? What content is grabbing their attention? Is there content that users are begging for and not finding? Armed with a few weeks' worth of data, I was ready to see take the search analytics tools for a spin.

So, what exactly are our community members typing into that little search bar? While most folks initially find their way to SAP Community pages via the big G, it's interesting to observe how their search behavior evolves once they've landed here. (Fun fact: In 2023, we averaged over 215k monthly searches – and now, with new tools, we're charting a new course.)

Let’s dive in and have a look at the top search terms* of the last almost-month.

* The Khoros instructional text reads:

The total number of search terms or phrases entered by users that returned search results; sorted by the number of times all users have entered those terms. This number includes all types of searches (community, category, board, TKB, and user).

This metric approximates user behavior and therefore excludes almost all nonhuman requests (Web crawlers, robots, RSS feeds, REST API calls).

This metric also excludes related search behavior like filtering results, paging through search results, “instant” search, and autocomplete.

 

Cracking the top 10 we see SAP staples (“sap analytics cloud,” “abap,” “ewm”) mingling with seasonal queries such as “w2” and the fashionable "generative ai".

 

All those high-level SAP keywords at the top are indicative of navigational intent, suggesting a typical search behavior and comfort level with SAP branded terms… or perhaps something less optimistic, like difficulty finding information or confusing structure.

 

I’m sure nobody has an opinion on that.... hey look, word cloud! 

 

What does it tell you?

 

 

For any given query I’m able to drill in and see which posts drove the most traffic, as well as related expressions. The top post for the query "generative ai" was a blog post about generative AI hub.

 

Dead-end Queries

On the other side, what about those searches that hit a dead end?

First, a curious revelation: Some straddle both lists, caught between a search query and the void of unfulfilled results. Apparently, you can enter a search term and apply filters until you've faceted yourself out of luck – a plight even the mighty "abap" cannot escape.

If there’s one point of this, it should be to investigate this data to find ways to bridge the gap between your questions and the answers you seek.

To that end, I downloaded the results as CSV and clustered similar searches, for example a very popular search involving NULL results. It’s a lot of cryptic number strings and wingdings. Sometimes names and emails (which I've kindly blotted out)! And even some cursing – "s/4hana cloud" 😉.

Even if this storm cloud doesn’t exactly make it clear what content might be missing, it’s a start.

These musings are just the beginning of the journey. Join the search party and share your thoughts and insights – together, we'll uncover the secrets that lie within!

entry.title: Sydney Stammtisch Recap 2402
entry.content:
As promised the most recent Sydney Stammtisch was at a new venue, The Orient Hotel in The Rocks

I thought it worked well. It was pleasant to be on the terrace, easy to get served at the adjacent bar and it was easy to chat because the music wasn't too loud

But what do you think?  If you came along let us know in the comments if we should pick this venue again in future

I found the evening quite relaxing because we had a good turnout almost right from the start.  We had quite a few regulars (where would we be without them?) but also some new faces, which is terrific. I didn't really get to chat to everyone as much as I would have liked, but then there is always next time...

..likely that will be in May, after the next school hols.  Add a comment below if you have venue suggestion for the next event

#SAPStammtischSYD

entry.title: Looking Back at the Success of SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2024
entry.content:
Last week, on February 17th, the SAP Inside Track Bengaluru once again proved its mettle as an unparalleled hub for knowledge sharing and networking. This event extended a warm welcome to over 1400 attendees, 120+ speakers & 30+ volunteers offering an enriched platform where learnings were amplified and connections were strengthened.. Personally, it's been the best SAP Inside Track that I've been a part of. This wouldn't have been possible without the hard work and dedication of everyone involved. In this blog post, I will share some of my personal highlights of the event while celebrating the team that made this successful event possible. So, let's get started.

Agenda Highlights

Our event took off with an energy-filled keynote from Sindhu Gangadharan (SVP & MD, SAP Labs India) which illuminated on the pivotal subjects of the SAP Community, Learning, Artificial Intelligence, and more. The event was nothing less than a feast for tech enthusiasts, featuring 56 deep-dive lecture sessions, ~120 speakers, 6 hands-on, and 3 interactive 'Ask Me Anything' booths. These sessions, running on eight parallel tracks, ensured a wide range of topics covered, providing a comprehensive learning experience for all the attendees. I was fortunate to lead the content team where I aided in strategizing and designing the content, working collaboratively with the team. We had 7 tracks of lecture sessions, covering SAP BTP, Application Development - SAP Build, CAP & RAP, Integration, Data Analytics, Automation, SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, SAP UI5, Fiori & Many other functional topics. Check out our agenda here: https://sitblrwebsite.vercel.app/


(Ask me Anything Strategy Corner)

Did I just miss mentioning the Artificial Intelligence?, it was intentional 😉, the highlight of this event is the Business AI, almost 25+ sessions from this event had the flavor of AI in it, in all the tracks. Let's talk about it next 🙂

Advent of AI Discovery Paths:

A significant highlight was the launch of an AI Discovery Paths - a personalized AI journey depending on the attendee’s role selection. This facilitated attendees to select a path best suited to their roles, be it developer, consultant, architect, product manager, or admin. We appreciate Peeyush Chaurasiya for designing this, and thanks to Sindhu Gangadharan for giving us the idea to try this at this event before launching it at the upcoming SAP DCOM.
Check them out here: https://sitblrwebsite.vercel.app/paths

SAP BTP at the Epicenter:


The SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) found significant prominence at this event. There were a total of 34 lecture sessions from SAP Employees, Customers & Partners on topics ranging from Application Development, Artificial intelligence, Automation, Integration and Data-to-Value, and also four hands-on practical interactions, and two specialized 'Ask Me Anything' booths that offered attendees valuable insights from SAP Product Experts. The BTP diamond game, held thrice for customers and partners, made its mark as an engaging highlight.

Feedback that Motivates:

The feedback from the attendees was overwhelmingly positive, clocking an impressive average of 4.56 out of 5 from over 300 entries across all the sessions. The AI sessions, specifically, garnered rave reviews. We also gathered the event experience feedback that received noteworthy scores, with an average feedback of 4.57 from about 200 entries. It was heartening to learn that many attendees found the event to be a valuable platform for learning about SAP BTP, AI, and SAP S/4HANA, among other trending topics, and wanted us to organize these events more often. Of course, there were a few requests for improvements which will definitely consider for the future but considering this as a free event.

Kudos to the Team:

The immense success of the event would not have been possible without the help of all our volunteers dedicated at every step. Their dedication and effort helped us execute one of the best SAP Inside Track events Bengaluru has ever seen. From content curation to track management, their invaluable assistance was present at every stage of the event. Check out this blog post from Shreshtha for more information: <Blog post in process>

Sponsors

A massive thank you to our sponsors, without whom the SAP Inside Track Bengaluru wouldn't have been possible. Their ample support, both financial and logistical, played a crucial part in the smooth running of the event. Not only did they provide us with the necessary backing to host a profuse range of sessions and interactive booths, but some also contributed their own expertise as speakers. Their support was invaluable in making this event a grand success, and we extend our heartfelt thanks to them all. (Primus Techsystems, S2 Integrators, SAP Concur & SAP Press).

Lastly, I want to express my sincere gratitude to Pushpendra Yadav for managing all logistics flawlessly, making the event a truly amazing experience, and Abhishek Chatterjee, the driving force behind this outstanding event. My appreciation extends to PVN Pavankumar for his invaluable support in strategising and reviewing the event’s content agenda. I would also like to express my deep gratitude toward Sindhu Gangadharan for her unwavering support to us in organizing these important events in this region.


(Mahesh, PVN Pavankumar, Pushpendra Yadav, Abhishek Chatterjee)

The End

To sum up, SAP Inside Track Bengaluru was more than just an event - It was a confluence of shared knowledge and networking, and fostering collaborative learning by brining together the SAP Community. I had a great time being part of the organizing & content team, which enabled me to learn to lot and of course the networking 😉 . Looking forward to the upcoming SAP Inside Tracks in this India.
